claim_boundary: These generated records are local, non-normative candidate audit controls. They do not close the handoff tasks, resolve independent review, satisfy an external release gate, certify a consumer or deployment, or authorize publication.
assumptions: Network attackers can read, inject, replay, reorder, delay, duplicate, or drop traffic.
Credentialed peers can claim the wrong entity, role, plane, session, or authority.
Processes, routers, and hosts can crash, restart, exhaust resources, or lose replies.
The body host and physical safety system are outside NCP's certification boundary.
Local source-tree tests cannot supply independent, live, duration, signed, or publication evidence.
trust_boundaries: decoded payload claim to verified transport principal
transport route to live session and plane
commander intent to body-owned final actuator authority
simulation output to scientific or physical claim
legacy wire and consumer repository to native 1.0
source tree to installed package and public registry

current_behavior_dimensions.current_behavior: Bounded canonical JSON, exact routes, profiles, packages, and immutable migration inputs.
Raw resource limits precede schema and semantic validation; unknown authorizing values fail closed.
Session generations, stream high-water marks, authority terms, idempotency records, and safety latches are explicit.
Lifecycle, authority, reset, reconnect, and fail-safe transitions are closed and non-reviving.
Replies, receipts, frames, diagnostics, and audit events retain exact identity and scientific boundaries.
Registered codes classify failure; free text never grants success.
Envelope, queue, retry, task, and diagnostic budgets are finite before expensive work.
UTC evidence is separated from local monotonic control deadlines; equality boundaries fail closed.
Payload claims require verified transport-principal, route, plane, and live-generation binding.
Unknown mutation outcomes stay unknown; released baselines and evidence identities are immutable inputs.
Plane queues, callback ownership, cancellation, ambiguous delivery, and shutdown have explicit local behavior.
HEAD remains an unreleased candidate; security, safety, science, scale, consumers, and publication are not certified.

mandatory_counterfactuals.scenario: correct version string with wrong contract or algorithm digest
clean synthetic result under a nonrepresentative distribution
timeout or capacity exhaustion followed by convenience fallback
feature unification activating a privileged or experimental path
partial migration accepted through default values
crash between decision or output and durable evidence
simple but odd input unlikely to resemble familiar examples

threats.title: Payload identity is mistaken for authentication
threats.boundary: transport principal to NCP identity claim
threats.asset_or_claim: principal, entity, role, and plane authorization
threats.actor_or_trigger: remote peer with a syntactically valid claim
preconditions: a payload reaches an NCP-aware transport boundary
threats.misuse_or_failure: The receiver trusts IdentityClaim without binding it to the verified transport principal.
threats.accepted_case: A development-loopback message remains visibly unauthenticated and non-production.
threats.rejected_case: A production message without callback-visible verified principal binding is rejected before state change.
threats.impact: An attacker can impersonate a commander, body, observer, or operator.
prevention: production-secure open fails closed until binding exists
threats.failure_response: Reject the message or startup; do not infer identity from payload fields.
threats.residual_risk: The stable Zenoh API still lacks callback-visible authenticated peer identity; the live campaign is NOT RUN.

threats.title: Authenticated peer exceeds enrolled authority
threats.boundary: verified principal to exact entity, role, plane, session, and lease
threats.asset_or_claim: least privilege and final actuator authority
preconditions: transport authentication succeeded
the peer requests another plane or entity
threats.misuse_or_failure: Authentication alone is treated as permission to publish or mutate.
threats.accepted_case: An enrolled commander with the exact live lease may issue an admitted mutation.
threats.rejected_case: A valid certificate for the wrong entity, role, plane, or lease is denied.
threats.impact: A credentialed peer widens its authority or actuates another plant.
threats.failure_response: Fail closed to denial or HOLD without changing authority state.
threats.residual_risk: Offline policy is tested, but live verified-principal enforcement remains unavailable.

threats.title: Stale generation or stream position is replayed
threats.asset_or_claim: freshness, high-water state, and restart fencing
threats.actor_or_trigger: prior holder, delayed network sample, or restarted publisher
preconditions: an old message remains well formed
the session or publisher has advanced
threats.misuse_or_failure: A correctly encoded stale frame refreshes a lease, watchdog, TTL, or output.
threats.accepted_case: The exact live generation and strictly increasing position are accepted.
threats.rejected_case: A retired generation, foreign epoch, duplicate, or lower sequence is rejected before callback or latch mutation.
threats.impact: Old authority or action is revived after restart or reordering.
threats.failure_response: Reject without refreshing any deadline or safety state.
threats.residual_risk: Process-crash, capture replay, rollover, and duration campaigns remain incomplete.

threats.title: Version text masks contract or algorithm drift
threats.boundary: wire compatibility and complete normative identity
threats.actor_or_trigger: peer or package with the right wire string and different semantics
preconditions: wire major is compatible
contract bytes or digest algorithm differ
threats.misuse_or_failure: A matching version string is treated as proof of identical semantics.
threats.accepted_case: The wire gate accepts compatible 1.x and reports the exact complete digest separately.
threats.rejected_case: Malformed versions fail; compact or complete digest drift is surfaced and cannot certify artifacts.
threats.impact: Peers silently disagree on security, safety, or canonicalization rules.
threats.failure_response: Reject malformed/incompatible wire and retain digest mismatch as non-certifying evidence.
threats.residual_risk: Independent artifact-bound verification and signatures remain external release evidence.

threats.title: Syntactically valid but contradictory state grants success
threats.asset_or_claim: closed lifecycle, error, authority, capability, and scientific states
preconditions: JSON and schema syntax are valid
threats.misuse_or_failure: Unknown, default, or contradictory values are interpreted as authority or success.
threats.accepted_case: A complete known state satisfying all cross-field invariants is admitted.
threats.rejected_case: Unknown enums, missing evidence, mismatched receipts, or contradictory flags fail closed.
threats.impact: Invalid combinations reach application or actuation state.
threats.failure_response: Return a registered error or non-authorizing HOLD state.
threats.residual_risk: All-language construction and independent mutation evidence remain incomplete.

threats.title: Resource exhaustion triggers permissive recovery
threats.boundary: untrusted bytes and transport work queues
threats.asset_or_claim: bounded parsing, queues, retries, and fail-safe behavior
threats.actor_or_trigger: malformed sender, slow consumer, flood, or exhausted host
preconditions: an ingress or queue reaches a configured limit
threats.misuse_or_failure: A timeout, overflow, or allocation failure bypasses validation or widens authority.
threats.accepted_case: Within-budget input is decoded before semantic allocation.
threats.rejected_case: Oversize, over-depth, queue-full, timeout, or cancellation states fail explicitly without a permissive fallback.
threats.impact: Denial of service becomes unauthorized delivery or invisible data loss.
threats.failure_response: Reject, count, HOLD, or terminate the affected transport; never grant success.
threats.residual_risk: Maximum-size CPU/RSS, hostile traffic, leak, and duration evidence is NOT RUN.

threats.title: Dependency feature unification activates excluded code
threats.asset_or_claim: reviewed Zenoh transport surface and advisory containment
threats.actor_or_trigger: downstream feature unification or dependency update
preconditions: a dependent enables defaults or transport compression
threats.misuse_or_failure: An excluded vulnerable or privileged feature enters the compiled surface silently.
threats.accepted_case: The reviewed lock and exact feature set keep defaults and compression disabled.
threats.rejected_case: Unexpected Zenoh defaults, compression, or unreviewed versions fail the dependency-exposure gate.
threats.impact: The shipped attack surface differs from the reviewed candidate.
prevention: publication hold on the retained Zenoh/lz4 advisory
threats.failure_response: Fail CI and keep publication blocked until the graph is patched and re-reviewed.
threats.residual_risk: The reviewed Zenoh path still locks the lz4_flex advisory while defaults and compression remain disabled under the resolved-feature guard; that specific residual blocks stable publication.

threats.title: Partial 0.8 migration invents missing context
threats.boundary: legacy wire input to native 1.0 state
threats.asset_or_claim: identity, security, session, authority, units, frames, and epistemic integrity
threats.actor_or_trigger: legacy gateway or copied consumer contract
preconditions: wire 0.8 lacks mandatory 1.0 context
threats.misuse_or_failure: Defaults or guessed values make an incomplete legacy message appear native 1.0.
threats.accepted_case: The labelled terminating translator maps only explicit authenticated channel requirements.
threats.rejected_case: Missing, null, mixed, reverse, transparent, or context-inventing translation is rejected.
threats.impact: A legacy peer gains native authority or produces scientifically mislabelled data.
threats.failure_response: Reject conversion and retain the source as legacy-unqualified input.
threats.residual_risk: The bounded repository validator checks explicit legacy units, frames, lineage, restart boundaries, and epistemic fields and excludes records needing unavailable native-1.0 authority or operation evidence; it emits no native-1.0 capture, while live consumer captures, independent clean-room replay, and installed-artifact migration certification remain NOT RUN.

threats.title: Crash separates mutation outcome from durable evidence
threats.asset_or_claim: at-most-once retry semantics and explicit unknown outcome
threats.actor_or_trigger: process crash, reply loss, cache eviction, or restart
preconditions: a mutation may have committed
the caller lacks a correlated terminal receipt
threats.misuse_or_failure: The caller retries and assumes success or re-executes because the result is absent.
threats.accepted_case: A matching authenticated terminal receipt is replayed from retained idempotency state.
threats.rejected_case: Unprovable outcomes return outcome_unknown and are never guessed or executed again by assumption.
threats.impact: A lifecycle mutation is duplicated or a false success is reported.
threats.failure_response: Return explicit unknown outcome and require reconciliation outside the mutation path.
threats.residual_risk: Durable server integration and process-crash campaigns remain NOT RUN.

threats.title: Synthetic output is promoted to scientific evidence
threats.actor_or_trigger: operator, paper, dashboard, or downstream dataset
preconditions: a clean simulation result resembles expected behavior
threats.misuse_or_failure: Simulation output is described as calibrated, experimental, representative, or a reproduction.
threats.accepted_case: Output remains explicitly simulation output with calibrated_posterior=false.
threats.rejected_case: Missing or upgraded epistemic flags and unsupported reproduction claims are rejected or removed.
threats.impact: Scientific conclusions exceed the model, data, and validation evidence.
threats.failure_response: Reject the message or narrow the public claim to raw simulation/control output.
threats.residual_risk: No external scientific validation or representative-distribution campaign exists.

threats.title: Protocol ESTOP is mistaken for physical safety certification
threats.asset_or_claim: plant-owned safe action and final actuator authority
threats.actor_or_trigger: deployment relying on mode text or transport success
preconditions: an ESTOP or HOLD message is accepted or published
threats.misuse_or_failure: Protocol state is treated as proof that a physical plant stopped safely.
threats.accepted_case: The body applies its content-addressed plant profile and independent interlock.
threats.rejected_case: A missing/mismatched profile, stale session, or malformed ESTOP is rejected without inventing a universal action.
threats.impact: Physical harm occurs despite a nominal protocol success.
threats.failure_response: Fail safe at the body and retain the absence of physical acknowledgement as an open limitation.
threats.residual_risk: No physical plant, universal safe action, or applied-command acknowledgement is certified.

threats.title: Production profile downgrades or starts partially configured
threats.asset_or_claim: mutual TLS, default-deny ACL, and no downgrade
threats.actor_or_trigger: misconfiguration, missing secret, expired certificate, or rushed operator
preconditions: production-secure is selected
threats.misuse_or_failure: A missing control silently falls back to plaintext, discovery, or development mode.
threats.accepted_case: A complete valid profile is precommitted and bound to an exact security-state digest.
threats.rejected_case: Partial TLS, insecure endpoints, hidden listeners, discovery, downgrade, or invalid files fail startup.
threats.impact: A deployment appears secure while accepting unauthenticated or overbroad traffic.
threats.failure_response: Abort startup and keep the live security gate NOT RUN.
threats.residual_risk: Principal binding is unimplemented and the live certificate/rotation/revocation campaign is NOT RUN.

threats.title: Route and payload refer to different live sessions
threats.boundary: actual transport key to payload session identity
threats.actor_or_trigger: misrouted, malicious, delayed, or copied data-plane sample
preconditions: the payload itself is well formed
threats.misuse_or_failure: A receiver trusts the payload session while delivery occurred on another route or generation.
threats.accepted_case: Route session, payload session_id, and current generation all match exactly.
threats.rejected_case: Cross-route, missing, stale, or foreign generation samples are rejected before callback and latch mutation.
threats.impact: One session observes or actuates another session's data.
threats.failure_response: Drop or reject the sample without repairing inbound identity.
threats.residual_risk: Authenticated multi-process cross-session evidence remains part of the NOT RUN live campaign.

threats.title: Ambiguous delivery reuses an action position
threats.asset_or_claim: single-use stream positions and fail-safe priority
threats.actor_or_trigger: transport error after a put may already have delivered
preconditions: put completion is ambiguous
threats.misuse_or_failure: The sender retries the same bytes or resumes Active without a new fail-safe position.
threats.accepted_case: Every attempted put consumes its position; a new logical fail-safe uses a new position.
threats.rejected_case: Same-position retry and Active while fail-safe delivery is pending are rejected.
threats.impact: A replay bypasses high-water checks or Active resumes after an unproved stop.
threats.failure_response: Block Active until a new fail-safe publishes successfully or declaration state is replaced.
threats.residual_risk: Fault injection against real routers and plants remains NOT RUN.

threats.title: Unicode, duplicate keys, or path tricks create cross-language ambiguity
threats.boundary: raw bytes to semantic object, route, or file path
threats.actor_or_trigger: odd-but-valid-looking JSON, Unicode, endpoint, or filesystem input
preconditions: different runtimes would otherwise normalize or collapse input differently
threats.misuse_or_failure: A duplicate, lone surrogate, unsafe integer, control character, or aliased path reaches semantics inconsistently.
threats.accepted_case: Canonical bounded input has one decoded key set, safe numbers, valid Unicode, and canonical paths.
threats.rejected_case: Duplicate/ambiguous/unsafe input is rejected before generic allocation, schema use, or file access.
threats.impact: Peers disagree on identity, digest, route, or authority while accepting the same bytes.
threats.failure_response: Reject the complete input; do not normalize it into an accepted identity.
threats.residual_risk: Duration fuzzing, sanitizers, and independent installed-peer differential evidence are NOT RUN.

threats.title: Package namespace or dependency provenance is misattributed
threats.boundary: source and package identity to public registry artifact
threats.actor_or_trigger: unrelated registry owner, typosquat, compromised dependency, or unsigned archive
preconditions: local package archives build successfully
threats.misuse_or_failure: Local packageability or a not-found registry response is treated as ownership or provenance.
threats.accepted_case: Exact intended namespaces, artifacts, checksums, SBOM, signatures, and provenance are independently verified.
threats.rejected_case: Unowned names, unsigned artifacts, unresolved advisories, and missing provenance remain failed release gates.
threats.impact: Users install unrelated or unreviewed code under an expected NCP name.
threats.failure_response: Do not publish; resolve ownership or rename every manifest, test, document, and consumer coherently.
threats.residual_risk: Registry control, final SBOM/provenance/signatures, and clean-room reproduction are absent.

threats.title: Candidate evidence is relabelled as release authorization
threats.boundary: local implementation evidence to public release claim
threats.actor_or_trigger: version-only relabel, stale metadata, missing signature, or incomplete checklist
preconditions: local and hosted tests pass
threats.misuse_or_failure: A green source-tree gate is described as a published, certified, or signed release.
threats.accepted_case: Local evidence remains bound to the candidate cut and explicit unresolved gates.
threats.rejected_case: A release tag or stable publication is blocked while any required evidence is missing, skipped, stale, or unsigned.
threats.impact: Public users rely on unsupported security, interoperability, performance, or safety claims.
threats.failure_response: Keep the candidate blocked and narrow or remove unsupported claims.
threats.residual_risk: Independent review, signatures, external gates, and publication receipts remain absent.

threats.title: Copied protocol files create a silent consumer fork
threats.boundary: NCP candidate to downstream runtime and descriptor
threats.asset_or_claim: one explicit consumer migration with exact installed behavior
threats.actor_or_trigger: partial repin, copied schema, or stale runtime implementation
preconditions: a consumer changes a version string or vendored file
threats.misuse_or_failure: The consumer is called native 1.0 without updating runtime, descriptor, fixtures, transport, and evidence together.
threats.accepted_case: A consumer pins one immutable NCP cut and passes installed-artifact plus live-transport certification.
threats.rejected_case: Movable refs, copied files, mixed wire values, incomplete descriptors, and unqualified profiles remain uncertified.
threats.impact: Cross-repository drift creates incompatible or unsafe ecosystem behavior.
threats.failure_response: Reject the compatibility claim and retain explicit cross-repository prerequisites.
threats.residual_risk: All six installed-artifact and live consumer certifications remain NOT RUN.
